mission.title: argo: A way to do something in hook after delete the Argo Application
mission.description: A way to do something in hook after delete the Argo Application. Requested by 69+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
It looks there's no way to do some post delete work when I delete an Argo Application, e.g.: to clean up something.

steps.title: Apply the fix for A way to do something in hook after delete the Argo
steps.description:
Probably not the neatest solution, but a workaround could be using `PostSync` hooks with some logic of identifying the nuked app. This would only work if the app-of-apps pattern is used and the parent application contains the hook definition. Native functionality would be nice as it'd greatly
```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  generateName: app-post-delete-notification-
  annotations:
    "helm.sh/hook": post-delete
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: post-delete-notification
          image: curlimages/curl
          command:
            - "curl"
            - "-X"
            - "GET"
            - "http://url"
      restartPolicy: Never
  backoffLimit: 2
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "A way to do something in hook after delete the Argo" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Probably not the neatest solution, but a workaround could be using `PostSync` hooks with some logic of identifying the nuked app. This would only work if the app-of-apps pattern is used and the parent application contains the hook definition. Native functionality would be nice as it'd greatly reduce complexity.

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job

            - "http://url"
      restartPolicy: Never
  backoffLimit: 2

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
